content: Hey guys! Is there any documentation on the binding between rust and the C kernel API

Hey guys! Is there any documentation on the binding between rust and the C kernel API

The best I could find so far regarding bindings and abstractions is Miguel's Mentorship Session: Rust for Linux: Writing Safe Abstractions & Drivers

Mohamed Aziz Hassene said:

Hey guys! Is there any documentation on the binding between rust and the C kernel API

What exactly are you looking for? How to write new bindings? How to use them?
(deleted)

Benno Lossin said:

Mohamed Aziz Hassene said:

Hey guys! Is there any documentation on the binding between rust and the C kernel API

What exactly are you looking for? How to write new bindings? How to use them?

I'm looking on how to use them.
Since the bindings are just the C api, you will have to look at the respective kernel documentation.
But using the bindings directly is not intended for drivers, if you want to write a driver (and upstream it) then you should consider writing abstractions for the systems you want to use
Which kernel API do you need to use?

I'm looking to use the cpu api where I want to get some info like vendor, frequency, number of cores something like that.
Note: I'm just experimenting some things
If you are just experimenting, then it's fine to just call the bindings.
for example if I want to use the core::arch::x86_64::__cpuid_count() function how should I use it
but that is just a function from the core library, you should be able to just call it, no?

Mohamed Aziz Hassene said:

for example if I want to use the core::arch::x86_64::__cpuid_count() function how should I use it

I think use corecrate API is akin to using it elsewhere; The core crate is always added to the extern prelude.

weikang guo said:

Mohamed Aziz Hassene said:

for example if I want to use the core::arch::x86_64::__cpuid_count() function how should I use it

I think use corecrate API is akin to using it elsewhere; The core crate is always added to the extern prelude.

I didn't get what you are trying to say
1701850135785.png this is a normal rust program(but not kernel)
1701850256952.png and this is kernel crate layout

1701850296279.png
RUST in kernel if want to use kernel API, through this way,
and kernel crate would include bindings crate（in rust/kernel/lib.rs) ,so you can use kernel api (like use kernel::bindings) ,the API definition created by bindgen in build/rust/bindings

Hi!
I used the cpuinfo_x86 struct through the binding module and cpu_detect() function to fill that struct but I got an error when compiling
"Error: modpost "cpu_detect" [samples/rust/<module_name>] undefined!
make[1]: ** [scripts/Makefile.modpost:136: Module.symvers] Error 1
make: *** [Makefile:1978: modpost] Error 2"
Does this mean that the function does not have a binding to Rust
Bindings are only generated for stuff included in rust/bindings/bindings_helper.h

Ariel Miculas said:

Bindings are only generated for stuff included in rust/bindings/bindings_helper.h

But in the file bindings_generated.rs I found the function
Are you compiling a kernel module?
yes
then probably you need to export it using EXPORT_SYMBOL_GPL or something like that
can you call cpu_detect from a C kernel module?
yes from the C perspective yes but when switching the Rust I got the previous error
In the C code I have not found any EXPORT...(cpu_detect), so how do you call it in the C side?

Benno Lossin said:

In the C code I have not found any EXPORT...(cpu_detect), so how do you call it in the C side?
When I check the /proc/kallsyms file the cpu_detect function is there with T symbol which I think it means that the function is exported globaly
You're right, the function is exported globally.

When I check the /proc/kallsyms file the cpu_detect function is there with T symbol which I think it means that the function is exported globaly

Symbols may be global yet not exported for modules. Please try calling it from a C module and you should see that the build fails.
Is there a way around that?
